meta_title: For Founders: user onboarding to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: User Onboarding to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with project stagnation. Learn to build high-converting user onboarding flows using Antigravity and Claude Code to drive SaaS revenue.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' trap is actually a lack of product-market fit validation. Build a high-converting user onboarding flow that forces users to define their pain points immediately. By shipping a specialized onboarding tool, you solve your own lack of direction while building a B2B asset companies will pay for.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank terminal, the cursor blinking with rhythmic indifference. You have the technical stack, the compute power, and the ambition, but the 'what' remains elusive. Most founders mistake this for a lack of creativity; it is actually a lack of friction. Without a clear path to value, even the most elegant code becomes digital debris.

True innovation isn't found in the abstract—it is forged in the onboarding experience. By building a sophisticated, AI-driven onboarding module, you transform generic users into power users within minutes. This isn't just about UI; it's about architecture that identifies user intent, automates data collection, and validates your business model before you write a single line of feature code.

steps.title: Architecting the Intent Engine
steps.description: Define the schema that captures user pain points. We need a structure that categorizes intent so we can serve hyper-personalized onboarding paths.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a TypeScript interface and Zod schema for a multi-step onboarding flow that captures user role, current pain points, and desired outcome metrics.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file src/types/onboarding.ts --prompt 'Generate Zod schemas based on the intent engine requirements.'

steps.title: Building the Adaptive UI
steps.description: Create a state-driven onboarding component that dynamically renders steps based on previous user input.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React component using Framer Motion that renders a dynamic step-based onboarding form. Include logic to skip or insert steps based on the user's initial selection.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file src/components/OnboardingFlow.tsx --prompt 'Implement the dynamic rendering logic for the onboarding form.'

steps.title: Integrating AI-Driven Personalization
steps.description: Use an LLM to analyze the onboarding responses and generate a 'Success Roadmap' for the user instantly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Node.js service that takes onboarding JSON data and returns a personalized 30-day success roadmap using OpenAI's API.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file src/services/roadmap.ts --prompt 'Write the service to generate personalized roadmaps based on user input.'

steps.title: Telemetry and Friction Analysis
steps.description: Install telemetry hooks to identify where users drop off, allowing for rapid iteration of the onboarding funnel.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a logging middleware that tracks user time-per-step and abandonment rate in the onboarding flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file src/middleware/analytics.ts --prompt 'Add telemetry hooks to track onboarding funnel drop-offs.'

steps.title: Deploying the MVP Feedback Loop
steps.description: Package the onboarding module as a standalone service to test its value proposition against real user data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a CI/CD pipeline script to deploy this onboarding module as a micro-service, ensuring it can be consumed by multiple SaaS apps.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file deploy.sh --prompt 'Create a deployment script for the micro-service architecture.'

pro_tips: Never ask for a password during onboarding; keep the barrier to entry at zero until the user experiences the 'Aha!' moment.
Use progressive profiling: ask for the most critical data point first, and save the secondary details for later interactions.
Embed a 'skip' option on every screen to respect user intent and identify which parts of your flow are actually redundant.
Treat onboarding as a product, not a feature; version it, A/B test the copy, and iterate based on conversion metrics.
If the user takes longer than 90 seconds to onboard, your UI is either too complex or your value proposition is unclear.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is live, monitor the drop-off rates for 48 hours. If abandonment is above 30%, use Claude Code to run a sentiment analysis on your onboarding copy and refine the call-to-action buttons for higher conversion.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting the 'no ideas' myth paralyze your potential. By treating the onboarding experience as the primary product, you create a scalable asset that drives revenue and clarifies your technical direction. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
